def: The act of entering a country.
The times during which a shop or office is open.
Water that is clean and safe to drink.
Water that falls from the clouds in drops.
Very warm temperature; hot weather.
A measure of how hot or cold something is.
The loud rumbling sound that follows lightning.
The curved band of colours seen when the sun shines through rain.
With plenty of bright sunshine.
With a lot of rain.
With strong moving air.
With strong winds and rough weather.
A light, gentle wind.
Snow falling from the sky.
A report predicting what the weather will be.
The usual weather of an area over a long period.
For small balls of ice to fall from the sky.
To become cooler; also to calm down.
Covered in fog, so that it is hard to see far.
Cold enough for frost to form; icy.
A short coat worn over other clothes.
Coverings worn on the feet.
Soft coverings worn on the feet inside shoes.
A covering worn on the head, often with a brim.
A long piece of cloth worn around the neck for warmth.
Coverings for the hands, with a part for each finger.
Clothes worn next to the skin under other clothes.
A fastener with two rows of teeth that lock together.
Feeling unhappy or full of sorrow.
Feeling very eager and full of energy.
Feeling tired and impatient because nothing interests you.
Extremely tired, with no energy left.
Feeling pleased about something you or others have done.
Unhappy because you fear losing someone's love or attention.
Feeling sudden wonder because something unexpected happened.
Not truthful; likely to lie or cheat.
Deep in thought; quietly reflective.
Behaving as if you are better than others.
Not willing to wait calmly.
Calm and free from stress or worry.
Not able to be trusted or depended on.
Pleasant and attractive in manner.
Expecting good things to happen.
Expecting bad things to happen.
A large wild cat with orange fur and black stripes.
A wild animal like a large dog that hunts in packs.
A bird of prey active at night, with large eyes.
A clever sea mammal known for its friendly nature.
A very large sea mammal, the biggest animal on earth.
A large fish with sharp teeth that hunts in the sea.
A large reptile with strong jaws that lives in rivers.
A huge grey animal with a long trunk and tusks.
An African animal like a horse with black and white stripes.
An Australian animal that hops and carries young in a pouch.
A slow reptile with a hard protective shell.
A small jumping animal that lives in and near water.
A furry animal that builds dams across streams.
A sea animal with a hard shell and claws that walks sideways.
A long, thin animal with no legs that lives in soil.
A furry river animal that swims well and eats fish.
A soft sea animal with a jelly-like body and stinging tentacles.
A tall pink water bird with long legs.
A solid food made from milk, often eaten on bread.
A warm liquid dish made by cooking ingredients in water or broth.
An alcoholic drink made from grapes.
Instructions for preparing a dish; also a doctor's prescription.
A thick liquid used for cooking or in machines.
A sour liquid used to flavour or preserve food.
The meat of cattle.
The meat of a pig.
Edible sea creatures such as shellfish and prawns.
A strong-smelling bulb used to flavour food.
A sweet spread made from fruit boiled with sugar.
A sweet, sticky food made by bees.
A mixed drink, usually containing alcohol.
The list of food and drinks a restaurant offers.

A country in central Europe, south of Germany.
A mountainous country in central Europe.
A country in western Europe.
A country in south-western Europe.
A country in southern Europe, shaped like a boot.
A country that is part of the United Kingdom.
The United States of America, a large country in North America.
The largest country in the world, spanning Europe and Asia.
A large country in East Asia with the world's biggest population.
The largest country in South America.
A country in North America, south of the United States.
A small, wealthy country on the Arabian Peninsula.
A tiny country between Switzerland and Austria.
A country lying across both Europe and Asia.
A country in south-eastern Europe with many islands.
A country in western Europe known for its flat, low land.
A country in northern Europe, part of Scandinavia.
A northern European country known for its fjords.
A country in central Europe, east of Germany.
A country in central Europe.
A country in central Europe.
A country in south-eastern Europe.
A country in south-eastern Europe on the Black Sea.
A country in south-eastern Europe on the Adriatic coast.
A landlocked country in south-eastern Europe.
A country in south-western Europe, west of Spain.

A Scandinavian country in northern Europe.

An island country in western Europe.

A country in north-east Africa, home to the ancient pyramids.

A country that is also a continent, in the southern hemisphere.
A large country in North America, north of the United States.
A country in southern South America.
A country at the southern tip of Africa.

A country in the Middle East, formerly called Persia.
A large country on the Arabian Peninsula.

A country in South-East Asia.
A South-East Asian country made up of many islands.
